title: WidthTiers
description: Schema for subject/band width tiers lookup files. Flat by descriptor code. Renderer merges with node map files at runtime via code key. One file per subject/band. Consistent naming with node_schema_v2.json.

generation_rules.description: Traceability — records which rules were used to generate this file.

primary_width_axis.description: Primary axis used for width variation in this file.

minimum_width.description: Definition of the minimum width tier for this file.

typical_width.description: Definition of the typical width tier for this file.

wider.description: Definition of the enrichment width tier for this file.

elaborations.description: Note on how elaborations were used as source data.

standards.description: Flat lookup keyed by curriculum descriptor code.

strand.description: Optional. Curriculum strand label. e.g. 'Understanding', 'Processes and Production'. Free text — matches node_schema_v2 strand field.

width_axis.description: Primary axis driving width variation. phenomena_contexts = number of phenomena or real-world contexts (primary for Understanding strand). task_contexts = range of tasks or application contexts (primary for Processes strand). depth_fallback = used only when breadth is not meaningfully variable.

minimum_width.description: Minimum construction required to certify this standard. Set by the command term in the descriptor — the non-negotiable floor. For Understanding: minimum phenomena/contexts. For Processes: encounter/recognise level in narrowest context.

typical_width.description: Full descriptor scope grounded in most common elaboration cases. What a well-taught student in a standard class would reach.

wider.description: Enrichment directions — extended phenomena, contexts or cases. Must add phenomena or context — not depth-on-fewer-phenomena. Example directions only, not exhaustive or prescriptive.

elaborations.description: Curriculum elaborations verbatim. Source data for the width tiers.

width_notes.description: Optional. Flags non-standard decisions — e.g. depth_fallback used because breadth is not variable, or enrichment inferred beyond elaborations.

_naming.minimum_width: Floor — set by descriptor command term. Consistent with node_schema_v2 minimum_width.
_naming.typical_width: Standard scope — typical elaboration range. Consistent with node_schema_v2 typical_width.
_naming.wider: Enrichment directions array. Consistent with node_schema_v2 wider.
_naming.strand: Replaces standard_type (U/P/H/I) — free text, subject-agnostic. Optional for traceability.

_migration.from_v1: science_y7_width_tiers.json uses standard_type (U/P/H/I) — replace with strand free text when convenient. dt_width_7_8.json missing strand and width_axis fields — backfill when convenient. Field names minimum_width/typical_width/wider already correct in both files.

_notes.relationship_to_node_files: Width tiers files operate at standard level. Node files operate at node level. Both use minimum_width/typical_width/wider but at different granularity. Node-level width is the operative planning data. Standard-level width tiers provide teacher-facing context and govern the rules used to generate node-level width.
_notes.width_principle: Width creates the conditions for depth — it is not depth itself. Enrichment must add phenomena or context, never just harder questions about the same case.
_notes.file_naming: Follow pattern: {subject}_{band}_width_tiers.json e.g. science_y7_width_tiers.json, dt_y7_8_width_tiers.json, maths_y7_width_tiers.json
